str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Water supply; sewerage, waste management and remediation activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to water supply; sewerage, waste management and remediation activities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Activities of extraterritorial organizations and bodies
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to activities of extraterritorial organizations and bodies

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Public administration and defence; compulsory social security
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to public administration and defence; compulsory social security

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of valuables
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in valuables (e.g., precious metals, works of art).

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on production and imports less subsidies
str:Code.com:Description: Total taxes on production and imports less total subsidies.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on products
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies payable per unit of a good or service.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Education, human health and social work
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to education, human health and social work

str:Code.com:Name: External balance of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between exports and imports of both goods and services. A positive value indicates a trade surplus; a negative value a trade deficit.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Professional, scientific and technical activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to professional, scientific and technical activities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Information and communication
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to information and communication

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Purchases of goods and services from abroad.

str:Code.com:Name: Final consumption expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. Final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services sold to buyers in other countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Financial and insurance activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to financial and insurance activities

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in inventories
str:Code.com:Description: Value of entries into inventories less the value of withdrawals and less the value of any recurrent losses of goods held in inventories during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes payable per unit of a good or service.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Real estate activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to real estate activities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Human health and social work activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to human health and social work activities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Arts, entertainment and recreation
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to arts, entertainment and recreation

str:Code.com:Name: Compensation of employees
str:Code.com:Description: Total remuneration, in cash or in kind, payable by an enterprise to an employee in return for work done by the latter during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Agriculture, forestry and fishing
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to agriculture, forestry and fishing

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Transportation and storage
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to transportation and storage

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP) production approach
str:Code.com:Description: The value of total output less total intermediate consumption plus any taxes less subsidies on products not already included in the value of output.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of services
str:Code.com:Description: Services provided by residents to non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Industry and construction
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to industry and construction

str:Code.com:Name: Consumption of fixed capital
str:Code.com:Description: The decline in the value of fixed assets due to wear and tear, obsolescence, or accidental damage (depreciation).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross fixed capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets during the accounting period, including certain specified expenditures on services that add to the value of non-produced assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net taxes on production
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on production less subsidies on production.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross national disposable income
str:Code.com:Description: Total income available for final consumption expenditure or saving.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross national income (GNI)
str:Code.com:Description: Total income earned by residents of an economy, including income from abroad.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of services
str:Code.com:Description: Services provided by non-residents to residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Gross capital formation reflects the acquisitions less disposals of produced assets for purposes of fixed capital formation, inventories or valuables.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Other service activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to other service activities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Mining and quarrying
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to mining and quarrying

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Construction
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to construction

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Industry and water supply
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to industry and water supply

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: Change in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: Change in the economic ownership of goods from non-residents to residents of the compiling economy, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Operating surplus and mixed income, gross
str:Code.com:Description: The surplus or deficit accruing from production to owners of capital (operating surplus) and to unincorporated enterprises (mixed income) before deducting consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Manufacturing
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to manufacturing

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Education
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to education

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Public administration, defence, education, human health and social work activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to public administration, defence, education, human health and social work activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Administrative and support service activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to administrative and support service activities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Other services
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to other services

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Trade, transport, accommodation and food
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to trade, transport, accommodation and food

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Accommodation and food service activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to accommodation and food service activities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross savings
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of disposable income not used for final consumption expenditure, before deducting consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Activities of households as employers of domestic personnel
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to activities of households as employers of domestic personnel

str:Code.com:Name: Net saving
str:Code.com:Description: Gross saving after deducting consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Net national income (NNI)
str:Code.com:Description: GNI minus depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Net capital transfers, Rest of the world
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers of ownership of assets or funds for investment purposes, net of any offsetting transfers. Non-resident entities or sectors external to the reporting country or economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Final consumption expenditure, Households
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. Final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households. Households are a person or a group of persons who share the same living accommodation, who pool some, or all, of their income and wealth and who consume certain types of goods and services collectively, mainly housing and food.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Business services
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to business services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current transfers, Rest of the world
str:Code.com:Description: All current transfers other than current taxes on income, wealth, etc., social contributions and benefits. In the external accounts, all current transfers other than personal transfers are included in other current transfers. Non-resident entities or sectors external to the reporting country or economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical discrepancy (expenditure approach)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between GDP calculated using the expenditure approach and other approaches.

str:Code.com:Name: Disposable income, net
str:Code.com:Description: Income available for final consumption expenditure or saving.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic income
str:Code.com:Description: Total income earned through the production of goods and services in the economy, consisting of remuneration of employees plus gross operating surplus plus gross mixed incomes plus taxes less subsidies on both production and imports

str:Code.com:Name: Final consumption expenditure, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. Final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Public administration and defence; compulsory social security
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to public administration and defence; compulsory social security

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Activities of extraterritorial organizations and bodies
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to activities of extraterritorial organizations and bodies

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply

str:Code.com:Name: Final consumption expenditure, Non profit institutions serving households
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. Final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households. Non-profit institutions primarily serving households, including charities and educational institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Education
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to education

str:Code.com:Name: Final consumption expenditure, Households and non profit institutions serving households (NPISH)
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. Final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households. Includes households and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in inventories, Private sector
str:Code.com:Description: Value of entries into inventories less the value of withdrawals and less the value of any recurrent losses of goods held in inventories during the accounting period. Broad category encompassing privately owned corporations and individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / net borrowing (-)
str:Code.com:Description: Net national income less final consumption expenditure.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Spending by households, government, and non-profits on goods and services for final consumption. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Collective consumption expenditure, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on services provided simultaneously to all members of the community or to all members of a particular section of the community. Collective final consumption expenditure is only measured for general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets: machinery and equipment
str:Code.com:Description: Acquisitions less dispos

str:Code.com:Name: Final consumption expenditure, Private sector
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. Final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households. Broad category encompassing privately owned corporations and individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of primary incomes, net / National income, net, Rest of the world
str:Code.com:Description: Balance of primary incomes after deducting consumption of fixed capital. Equivalent to Net National Income (NNI). Non-resident entities or sectors external to the reporting country or economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets non-residential structures
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in non-residential buildings and structures (e.g., factories, offices).

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets: residential structures
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in residential buildings and dwellings.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA), Activities of households as employers; undifferentiated goods- and services-producing activities of households for own use
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production. Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to activities of households as employers; undifferentiated goods- and services-producing activities of households for own use
